config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya demi “kepentingan masyarakat” yang sering kali ditafsirkan terlalu luas dan bisa disalahgunakan oleh penguasa atau mayoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan menghormati martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan dasar setiap orang. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang haknya dijaga, bukan dari individu yang mudah ditekan atas nama kepentingan umum.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to justify abuse, majoritarian pressure, or government overreach. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case; public safety, public health, and basic rules that keep everyone from harming each other do matter. The key is that any limit on individual rights should be narrow, justified, and subject to strong checks, not a blank check for society to override people whenever it wants. From my perspective, a healthy society protects individuals first, because that is what makes legitimate collective action possible in the first place.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahaya ketika “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu longgar untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan, menekan minoritas, atau menutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa oleh mayoritas maupun pemerintah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas dan perlindungan hukum yang kuat, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan orang per orang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang benar-benar sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang mampu melindungi hak individu sambil tetap mengatur kepentingan kolektif secara adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, history shows that once “the public good” becomes the trump card, it can excuse censorship, discrimination, and government abuse, especially against unpopular minorities. At the same time, I do not see rights as a license to ignore public safety or to harm other people, so some limits are necessary, but they should be tightly drawn and heavily accountable. To me, the burden should always be on society to prove why a right must be limited, not on individuals to justify why they deserve to keep it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai secara longgar untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan, menekan kelompok minoritas, atau menutup-nutupi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi, tetapi hanya secara sempit, jelas, dan diawasi ketat, bukan dijadikan alasan untuk mengorbankan orang per orang. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru lahir ketika hak dasar tiap warga dilindungi, lalu kepentingan kolektif diatur dengan adil tanpa menjadikan individu sebagai korban.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as a general principle, because from a U.S. perspective it has too much potential to justify government overreach and majoritarian abuse. I do think society can sometimes limit individual rights for real public safety or public health reasons, but those limits have to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. The burden should be on society to prove the necessity of the restriction, not on the individual to prove why their rights matter. In my view, a free society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the default, not as something society can override whenever it claims a collective interest.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
